tweets.text: We are promoting the freedom, dignity, and equality of all people - including LGBTQI+ persons. @TravelGov Acting Assistant Secretary Brownlee discusses proposed updates to our procedures for issuing official documents including U.S. passports. Learn more: https://t.co/9Z6f0nVYMs. https://t.co/ONmOTLbasX

At today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed @SecBlinken’s announcement regarding gender markers on U.S. passports and consular reports of birth abroad, as well as U.S. efforts to support COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing in South Africa. Read more: https://t.co/V8S0XRhZPK.

This #PrideMonth the Department made history by raising the Progress flag. We will continue to push for equity and the rights of all people. Read our latest DipNote for more on this year’s events. https://t.co/3wINczn915
Thanks to our colleagues @USEmbassyWarsaw and friends in Poland for this powerful reminder that #WordsMatter. #PrideMonth https://t.co/vIP2T89UVD

Christopher Johnson is a proud, openly gay Foreign Service Officer of African and Nicaraguan descent. He’s worked with seasoned Department leadership who are committed to doing the work to create a more inclusive & equitable workforce. Thanks for making our team stronger. #Pride https://t.co/ONa2wbS8ta

Taylor Westfall is a Foreign Affairs Officer covering human rights in the Middle East, religious freedom, @UN human rights reviews & LGBTQI+ issues. She’s served in challenging environments including Iraq & Afghanistan. Thank you for your contributions to the Department. #Pride https://t.co/Ss0qDaAbCE

Daniel Buchman is a Foreign Service Officer who translated the story of a person in a local, marginalized community for the U.S. Ambassador to that country. This resulted in Embassy initiatives to address homelessness & discrimination. We thank you for empowering others! #Pride https://t.co/1hv0pnAyaN

Catriona, a Civil Service IT professional: “As a trans woman, the U.S. gives me rights that are hard to find in most other countries. My career is just one way that I try to give something back.” #Pride https://t.co/xqHKfZ66wa
Join @NMADMuseum today at 5 p.m. EDT as Asian American & Pacific Islander diplomats share their stories and contributions to American diplomacy. Learn more: https://t.co/QQ1f0vzHAn.

During National Immigrant Heritage Month, we honor the strength from the immigrants in America, celebrating the history and achievements of immigrant communities across our Nation. Read the full @POTUS proclamation: https://t.co/49lqnf13kk. https://t.co/j9Tc8RWvkQ

Frank Zhu, a Passport Specialist for the U.S. Department of State, born in China, says, “For me, growing up with understanding of another language and culture means greater access to information as well as perspectives.” #CelebrateImmigrants https://t.co/3D6ZFTTWiq

Yuliana Anderson grew up in Kyrgyzstan where she studied at a U.S.-funded university. Her first job after graduating was at an American Embassy. Now Yuliana is a U.S. citizen and is using her skills to serve at the State Department. https://t.co/DBkd7wlXce

Jerry Meras, a State Department Diplomatic Courier born in Cuba, on why we #CelebrateImmigrants: “This month is a celebration of the wealth of varied experiences and talents immigrants bring to the State Department, and society as a whole.” https://t.co/GgNTbeXZPN

During National Immigrant Heritage Month, we honor the generosity, innovation, and diversity of the generations of immigrants that have enriched our Nation. #CelebrateImmigrants https://t.co/wTpv56FIe3
With 2 million doses of the Pfizer vaccine donated to Peru, the United States is helping people in need. The more the world is vaccinated, the safer we all are. https://t.co/lnCNIvgGSG

Klaudia Krueger emigrated from Austria. She is a Foreign Service Officer currently serving in the Bureau of Global Talent Management. As a part of the team, she’s contributed to our nation's heritage by bringing her Austrian culture & making a difference through public service. https://t.co/GPDWEFGQpd

We join @POTUS in recognizing the incredible contributions immigrants make to our society. Here at the U.S. Department of State we are proud to have native-Peruvian E. Giovanni Vizcardo L. on our team, who serves as an Educational Technologist. https://t.co/iGOdakIWif

Today, @StateDeputySpox noted the delivery of 2 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Peru, took a question on Special Envoy for Iran Malley’s talks, and called on the Palestinian Authority to respect the rights of Palestinians to protest peacefully. https://t.co/79k9NnITY2

Multilateralism is our best tool for tackling global challenges.  At the G20, @SecBlinken reinforced the U.S. commitment to strong coordination with partners and allies to address global challenges. https://t.co/aLoPWNrHSD https://t.co/k2aZqkzmhT

The G20 enables the United States to engage with the world’s largest economies to address global economic, political, and social issues. This includes combating the COVID-19 pandemic, tackling the climate crisis, and fostering economic recovery. https://t.co/aLoPWNa715 https://t.co/vixkePXUJF

"When we all work together, we’re a lot smarter and a lot stronger than any virus.” Watch Coordinator for Global COVID-19 Response and Health Security Gayle Smith explain how the U.S. is responding to the pandemic globally. For more, check out https://t.co/1BRAFjnTHE. https://t.co/hPhvLcXU9r

Christopher Marvil is a Civil Service professional working in Consular Affairs. Over the course of the pandemic, he worked tirelessly to schedule emergency passport services for U.S. citizens in life-or-death situations. Thank you for helping Americans in need. #Pride https://t.co/iYgUaXZ56K
U.S. diplomat Jose Vega who immigrated to the United States from Latin America, shares his story. #CelebrateImmigrants https://t.co/OuiGOmzMYd

The United States and Holy See are working together to raise countries’ collective ambition to address the climate crisis, including by raising and implementing national emissions reductions targets. https://t.co/QiiAiLLvkk https://t.co/mq5JmygM4D

On June 24, Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources Brian McKeon welcomed newly arrived refugees to Atlanta and met with a range of resettlement partners and elected officials to discuss the contributions of refugees to their communities. https://t.co/bdhudVaKJG https://t.co/hPnE30p28r

Locally Employed Staff keep our mission abroad running, supporting our U.S. Embassies & Consulates in countless ways. Learn about retired Liberian LES #HeroesofUSDiplomacy honoree Jenkins Vangehn’s contributions on June 29 at 11 a.m. EDT: https://t.co/rCE2AaR29K. https://t.co/lnMLzzvZi1

The United States and Holy See share a common commitment to the fight against human trafficking. The Holy See is a valued partner in the global effort to prevent this heinous crime. https://t.co/uowiZcYa1F

Participants also discussed the need for an immediate nation-wide ceasefire, the importance of fighting ISIS in Syria, and support for @UN-led efforts to implement all aspects of UN Security Council Resolution 2254.

Today in Rome, participants at the Syria Ministerial stressed the importance of providing life-saving humanitarian assistance for the Syrian people through the reauthorization and expansion of the @UN cross-border mechanism. https://t.co/5McL8iVdX1

Throughout June, we are #CelebratingImmigrants. Iva Ziza is a Foreign Service Officer born in Yugoslavia. She says, “Immigrant Heritage Month is a recognition and celebration of Americans who were not born on our soil, but whose stories nonetheless make America what it is.” https://t.co/U2iWNsvf8t

The U.S. values its cooperation with the Holy See and Catholic organizations on the ground to deliver humanitarian aid successfully and efficiently. Through our diplomatic relations with the Holy See, the United States benefits from its unparalleled global presence. https://t.co/5AydKlvcsq

The United States partnered with COVAX to ensure that 1.5 million doses of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine arrived in Honduras today. These vaccine doses are a part of our commitment to save lives and end the pandemic globally, and they do not come with strings attached. https://t.co/ce75ARqqWw
Subscribe to The Week at State, a weekly email newsletter from the Department of State. https://t.co/gNUp8OngPT https://t.co/prHXbwd5ft

On this Micro, Small, and Medium-sized Enterprises Day, we recognize the impact of COVID-19 and affirm our support for inclusive economic recovery. For more on our efforts to empower women-owned MSMEs, read the latest @StateGWI blog below. #MSMEDay21 https://t.co/KNgjtIU7Uk

The United States is Italy’s largest export market outside the EU. Our two-way trade in goods and services amounted to $80 billion last year. Our relationship has spurred collaboration in fields outside the economic realm, including security and space technology. https://t.co/Imtz5ss42X

Our relationship with Italy plays an important role in guaranteeing Transatlantic security, marshaling support for progress in Libya, and addressing shared threats in the Mediterranean region and around the world. https://t.co/2HZB7tzPVi

The United States and France are committed to the goals of the Paris Agreement. This includes working together to raise global climate ambitions and accelerate the transition to a clean energy economy to achieve net-zero emissions by 2050. https://t.co/uX0RqspRKy https://t.co/L9GUwgpWft
.@POTUS: President Macron and I stand together, ready to take on the toughest challenges we face. https://t.co/uX0Rqs8glY https://t.co/60K7gJAcHi

The United States and France share a deep trade and investment relationship. In total, we have invested more than $394 billion in each other's economies. https://t.co/uX0RqspRKy https://t.co/D03V5G2bt5

France is America’s oldest friend and Ally. Our relationship is built upon our shared commitment to the same values – democracy, human rights, the rule of law, security, and prosperity. https://t.co/uX0Rqs8glY https://t.co/IX1kJwsJSr

Today, we are combating common myths about substance use disorder and highlighting how @StateINL programs decrease stigma, increase evidence-based treatment tools, and provide support for those in need worldwide. #ShareFactsonDrugs #SaveLives https://t.co/0J1aO2ny2D

.@POTUS: During LGBTQI+ Pride Month, we recognize the resilience and determination of the many individuals who are fighting to live freely and authentically. https://t.co/2Pxc295Kwx [ https://t.co/RHyevG2uDm
Today, @DeputySecState Sherman and senior officials raised the progress pride flag at the Department. #PrideMonth https://t.co/Lnnd0uzEP5
Live now! Watch @DeputySecState and senior officials give remarks at the progress pride flag raising ceremony at the Department. #PrideMonth https://t.co/Z7aPo1RR7B

Today at 3:30 p.m. EDT @DeputySecState and other officials will deliver remarks at the progress flag raising ceremony at the Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7, and https://t.co/WlGxtkMPDK. https://t.co/jT0MNy2kix

Amanda Salazar is a @StateDeptDSS Special Agent. She’s visited 46 countries, across 5 continents on the Secretary's Protective Detail. One of her most memorable experiences was protecting @SecBlinken as he visited the Arctic Circle. The Department is grateful for your service! https://t.co/MzvQo5Lo58

We're proud of our LGBTQI+ colleagues here at the department and would like to celebrate their accomplishments and contributions. We are pleased to recognize them and thank them for their service this #PrideMonth2021.

.@POTUS: Democracies of the world are standing together to tackle the toughest challenges and the issues that matter most to our future: that we’re committed to leading with strength, defending our values, and delivering for our people. https://t.co/snj4H92zrx

The United States is using the power of our democracy, the ingenuity of American scientists & the strength of American manufacturing to beat the pandemic globally. Today 3 million COVID-19 vaccine doses arrived in Brazil as part of our commitment to save lives & end the pandemic. https://t.co/IBL2NMzFIb

.@SecBlinken on democracy and human rights: We joined the UN Human Rights Council as an observer. We put into effect the presidential proclamation that U.S. diplomacy protect the human rights of LGBTQI people worldwide. https://t.co/dPiULSYJ2x
Live now! From France, @SecBlinken participates in a moderated discussion with youth on democracy and human rights. https://t.co/uVnnmuYYs7

Today at 8:30 a.m. EDT (2:30 p.m. CEST) @SecBlinken will participate in a moderated discussion with youth on democracy and human rights. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/HgWcbr29qr. https://t.co/kNMrsoSxtc
.@SecBlinken: Human rights and dignity must stay at the core of the international order. https://t.co/O0p073u05L

The United States will continue to work with our partners and allies in calling on the PRC to cease its use of forced labor and atrocities against Uyghurs, who are predominantly Muslim, and members of other ethnic and religious minority groups.

The U.S. government has announced measures to address the People’s Republic of China’s (PRC) ongoing human rights abuses and use of forced labor in Xinjiang, particularly in the solar industry. https://t.co/e0qw58Mwlf

The U.S. and Germany are united in our goals of leading the world to develop innovative clean technologies, transforming energy systems, and achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions across global economies by or before 2050. https://t.co/Il2qmqoVIE https://t.co/F2T52GGAxz

Both the United States and Germany seek to ensure equitable access to safe and effective vaccines, with the U.S. announcing funding of $4 billion to COVAX and Germany announcing funding of €1.5 billion to COVAX and @WHO. https://t.co/HCIho0OZHu

The United States and Germany share a deep and mutually beneficial trade relationship. Since 2012, the United States and Germany have averaged roughly $170 billion in total trade. https://t.co/Il2qmqoVIE https://t.co/FAJ2S6GwyJ

The goal of the United States is a sovereign, stable, unified, and secure Libya with no foreign interference, and a democratically elected government that supports human rights and development and is able to combat terrorism within its borders. https://t.co/jrAuJlkDRS https://t.co/yek9AMaBeI
The U.S. implements programming across Libya to promote a peaceful political transition and to strengthen the foundations of a more unified Libya. https://t.co/jrAuJl32ti https://t.co/CTNMukKFhb
The United States strongly supports the progress the Libyan people have made towards an inclusive, negotiated political solution. https://t.co/jrAuJl32ti https://t.co/EYdPGOnoMN

.@POTUS recently announced how the remaining 55 million COVID-19 vaccine doses – of the United States’ initial pledge of 80 million doses – will be allocated and shared globally by the end of June 2021. Learn more below. https://t.co/0Z5jnfh8ob

Today, @SecBlinken gave opening remarks at the @UN_HRC virtual event “Defending the Lives of Transgender Women”: “We’re advancing LGBTQI+ rights worldwide as part of our foreign policy, at the direction of @POTUS.” https://t.co/KQLBoCUrK4

In our latest DipNote entry, a Foreign Service Officer shares her experiences and explains why efforts to expand diversity and inclusion are critical to American diplomacy. Read her story below. #ImmigrantHeritageMonth https://t.co/8Y4bkwdq3V

Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources Brian McKeon will travel to Atlanta and Clarkston, Georgia to underscore our commitment to welcoming refugees to the U.S. While there, he will meet with partners, volunteers, and resettled refugees. https://t.co/qfsHT19dDo

On June 24 at 7 p.m. EDT, join us to learn more about the work of @SciDiplomacyUSA Science Envoys. Register for our #PacificHealthSecurity event and join the discussion on global health and climate resilience below. https://t.co/eE2G4yQNlK https://t.co/GvKGCkG0Uy
Tomorrow at approximately 9 a.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will give virtual remarks at @UN_HRC discussion on “Defending the Lives of Transgender Women.” Register at https://t.co/LXbjdBz4K6. #HRC47

During today’s press briefing, @StateDeptSpox highlighted @ECAatState’s Global Teaching Dialogue online event featuring @ClimateEnvoy & reiterated the call for President Ortega and the Nicaraguan Government to release presidential contenders. More: https://t.co/uTAti1JJMH

The U.S.-Japan Conference on Cultural and Educational Interchange elevates and strengthens vital people-to-people ties. Thank you to everyone who has contributed to increasing understanding between our two countries through CULCON since 1961.

.@POTUS: The countries that take decisive action now to create the industries of the future will be the ones that reap the economic benefits of the clean energy boom that’s coming. https://t.co/o22CPSbejW

Let’s build on G7 outcomes with policies to drive decarbonization, invest in clean technologies, and together create a net-zero emissions future that leaves no one behind. https://t.co/9o1BZxTsAa https://t.co/AtpCXvVyPz

.@SecBlinken discusses the U.S. commitment to defend and advance the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons at home and around the world. “Human rights and democracy are intrinsically linked with stability, broad based prosperity, peace, and progress.” https://t.co/I7LgDZd3kp https://t.co/nZgR6ENx1z

During today’s briefing, @StateDeptSpox underscored the U.S. commitment to stand #withrefugees and discussed imposing costs on the Lukashenka regime, while reaffirming the U.S. stands with the people of Belarus in their aspirations for a democratic future. https://t.co/Gt0zTQUT5o

This week is #PollinatorWeek. Over 75% of the world’s food crops and 35% of global agricultural land depend entirely or in part on animal pollination. #Pollinators contribute directly to #foodsecurity and are key to conserving biodiversity. https://t.co/RV991I7vib https://t.co/DosswLGuWG

Today at approx. 1 p.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will participate in an @AtlanticCouncil virtual conversation with Jonathan Capehart on “Pressing for Equality: Engaging on LGBTQI+ Issues Around the World.” Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/WSDFkHUr8q. https://t.co/086t6E7uHA

The struggle to end violence, discrimination, criminalization, and stigma against LGBTQI+ persons is a global challenge that remains central to our commitment to promote human rights and fundamental freedoms for all people. #PrideMonth https://t.co/5HihXrHIue

.@POTUS: This #PrideMonth, we recognize the valuable contributions of LGBTQI+ individuals across America, and we reaffirm our commitment to standing in solidarity with LGBTQI+ persons worldwide in the ongoing struggle against discrimination and injustice. https://t.co/2Pxc29nlV7 https://t.co/a0IIhqJkXR

The EU is a critical partner in addressing the challenges we face around the world. Read more about how we are rebuilding, revitalizing, and raising the ambition of U.S.-EU Relations: https://t.co/eLHOHm3Ks5. https://t.co/5Jli0CLvGq

.@POTUS: Our alliances weren’t built by coercion or maintained by threats.  They’re grounded on democratic ideals and a shared vision of the future and where every voice matters. https://t.co/Qn8hf6KmPB https://t.co/6gyawDPLV7
.@POTUS: “To tackle this century’s most pressing challenges, we have to do it together...It requires coordinated, multilateral action.” https://t.co/Qn8hf6KmPB. https://t.co/34oGAmnLMZ
Subscribe to The Week at State, a weekly email newsletter from the Department of State. https://t.co/gNUp8OngPT https://t.co/aGBrYWXlnT

At the @NATO Summit with the leaders of Allied countries, @POTUS highlighted NATO’s work to improve the collective ability to defend against and counter threats, an enduring commitment to the people of Afghanistan, & progress on the NATO 2030 agenda. More: https://t.co/RRr4OjuClv

.@POTUS: The U.S. commitment to Article 5 of the @NATO Treaty is rock solid and unshakable. It’s a sacred commitment. NATO stands together. That’s how we’ve met every other threat in the past. It’s our greatest strength as we meet our challenges of the future. https://t.co/TItuIV8rKj

This Father’s Day, we celebrate the fathers and father figures for their generosity, honesty, sacrifice, and support. Thank you to all the dads who are tirelessly committed to building a better future for our families, friends, and communities across the globe. #HappyFathersDay https://t.co/mIuwrh8Lko
.@SecBlinken: "Refugees are welcome in the United States of America." https://t.co/Ep0phWYsKc

On World Refugee Day, we reaffirm our commitment to stand together #withrefugees to create a more inclusive world. The U.S. has announced that the refugee resettlement program is being restored, offering hope and safe haven to those fleeing persecution. https://t.co/AIUoDXCNaZ
.@SecBlinken: We must sustain the fight against systemic racism and oppression every day. https://t.co/kxfKYKMXDX https://t.co/GWeBki5U2Y

.@SecBlinken: It is also a day to honor the extraordinary contributions of the Black community to our nation throughout American history in the face of ongoing adversity and systemic racism. https://t.co/kxfKYKMXDX

.@SecBlinken: #Juneteenth reminds us of our responsibility to grapple with the enduring legacy of slavery and to tackle the barriers to full equity and justice for Black people in our country and around the world.

On #Juneteenth we honor the contributions, strength, and resilience of the Black community. Juneteenth is a critical day in American history and a reminder of how freedom and justice are delayed for far too many. https://t.co/3uITrT4Pza https://t.co/fKhiwvnmgb

On this International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict, we remain committed to preventing and responding to conflict-related sexual violence (CRSV). For more on U.S. efforts to address CRSV, read the latest blog entry from @StateGWI: https://t.co/3qUeNCwbXM

On this day in 1865, enslaved African Americans of Galveston, Texas received word of their freedom, that had been purposefully withheld for 2+ years after the Emancipation Proclamation. On #Juneteenth we continue to fight against systemic racism & build a freer, stronger country. https://t.co/TYvnCwF0IQ

On #Juneteenth we honor the contributions, strength, and resilience of the Black community. Juneteenth is a critical day in American history and a reminder of how freedom and justice are delayed for far too many. https://t.co/3uITrT4Pza https://t.co/6R4pLz9Re5

COVID-19 knows no borders and ensuring our neighbors can contain the virus is critical to stopping the spread of COVID-19 around the globe. This afternoon, 1M vaccine doses arrived in Canada as part of the U.S. commitment to lead the world in bringing an end to the pandemic. https://t.co/ZxG9lfth58

During the #NATOSummit, @POTUS reaffirmed the enduring Transatlantic bond through @NATO and underscored the United States’ ironclad commitment to Article 5 (an attack on one is an attack on all). Read more on U.S. efforts to revitalize the Alliance: https://t.co/XF7tHOSc1v.

The Transatlantic relationship is built on a foundation of shared democratic values. By deepening partnerships with democratic Allies, @NATO enhances our ability to strengthen the rules-based international order. https://t.co/XF7tHOSc1v. https://t.co/qZP8pHdlW7

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeptSpox discussed new humanitarian assistance for the people of Venezuela and provided an update on our global COVID-19 response. Read the transcript for more. https://t.co/u7R4Sl8Bq7

.@POTUS: Throughout our history, wave after wave of immigrants have enriched our Nation and made us better, stronger, more innovative, and more prosperous. #CelebrateImmigrants https://t.co/OV5Bvi6esr

During National Immigrant Heritage Month, we honor the generosity, innovation, and diversity of the generations of immigrants that have enriched our Nation. #CelebrateImmigrants https://t.co/XoDDx17VWq

.@POTUS on attacking the COVID-19 virus globally: This is about our responsibility — our humanitarian obligation to save as many lives as we can — and our responsibility to our values. https://t.co/T60A8Lydb2

.@POTUS on donating COVID-19 vaccine doses: “We value the inherent dignity of all people. In times of trouble, Americans reach out to offer help and to offer a helping hand. That’s who we are.” https://t.co/BOPrFcrlpn https://t.co/xWtPtoUAhJ

Today marks the 10th anniversary of the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights. Businesses should help promote values that support democratic principles, including respect for human and labor rights. Learn more: https://t.co/1qYq7RD0Q4.

.@POTUS: “America will be the arsenal of vaccines in our fight against COVID-19.” Read President Biden’s full remarks on the United States’ historic step to purchase & donate 500 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to combat the pandemic globally here: https://t.co/BOPrFcrlpn. https://t.co/DvcYITxu7R

On June 15, 1.35M doses of COVID-19 vaccine arrived in Mexico, part of the 80M doses that the U.S. will share with the world by the end of June. We will continue to work together as partners, neighbors, and friends to end COVID-19 globally. https://t.co/iHK2eispN4

The United States will purchase and donate 500 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to the world by June 2022, with the first 200 million doses being delivered by the end of 2021 through COVAX. Together, we will lead the world out of this pandemic. More: https://t.co/WmS1PD8BLT. https://t.co/zsdhaeM9RN

The Artemis program will land the first woman and the first person of color on the surface of the Moon. While the Artemis program will be led by @NASA, it will be a truly international effort. And Brazil will be an important part of it.

.@SecBlinken: Brazil & the U.S. have a long history of cooperation in scientific discovery. Now we will carry that partnership into a new era of space exploration together. Artemis will establish the sustainable human exploration of the solar system. https://t.co/eF9p12ISOs https://t.co/Q752aIan3F
